zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanette Murphy於1480年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾時，她的父親Timothy Murphy20歲，母親Mary O'Connor20歲。她於1515年與John Halsnoth Harsnett在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1556年在英國賀特福爾享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: Murphy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Liam, Aileen, Kieran, Brigid, Donal, Siobhan, Eamon, Aidan, Dermot, John Patrick, Declan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murchadha ‘descendant of Murchadh’, a personal name composed of the elements muir ‘sea’ + cath ‘battle’, i.e. ‘sea warrior’. This was an important family in Tyrone. For the surname with mac, see also McMurrough (Leinster).
English: variant of Morphey (see Morphew ).

variants.name: McMurrough
meanings: Irish:

Anglicized form (in Leinster) of Mac Murchadha ‘son of Murchadh’, a personal name meaning ‘sea warrior’. The spelling gh indicates the original guttural fricative sound of the internal -ch-. Elsewhere, in Roscommon and Tyrone, the usual Anglicized form was Murphy (the same phonetic change as in English laugh, cough etc.). Diarmait Mac Murchadha (Dermot McMurrough) was intermittently king of Leinster from 1126–71. After being deposed in 1166 he requested help from Henry II to reclaim his kingdom, which resulted in the Norman invasion of Ireland in 1169 under the leadership of Richard de Clare, Earl of Pembroke (known as ‘Strongbow’), an event that brought many Anglo-Norman surnames to Ireland. A powerful Irish family of this lineage were also known as Kavanagh , after Dermot's son.

Anglicized form (in Leinster) of Mac Murchadha ‘son of Murchadh’, a personal name meaning ‘sea warrior’. A powerful family of this name were also known as Kavanagh . The spelling gh indicates the original guttural fricative sound of the internal -ch-. Elsewhere (especially in Roscommon and Tyrone) the usual Anglicized form of the Irish name was Murphy .

variants.name: Morken
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads so named, from the definite form of Old Norse mǫrk ‘outlying pasture, wood’.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murcháin ‘descendant of Murchán’, a diminutive of the personal name Murchadh (see Murphy ).

variants.name: McMurphy
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Murchadha, a patronymic from the personal name Murchadh ‘sea warrior’, from muir ‘sea’ + cath ‘battle’. Compare Murphy , McMorrow , McMurdo .

variants.name: Murphrey
meanings: Probably Irish: variant of   Murphy  .

variants.name: Morphis
meanings: Irish: variant of   Murphy  .

variants.name: Morphy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Augusto, Jaime, Javier, Luis, Patricio.  Irish Donovan.
Scottish: variant of   Murphy  .
English (northern): variant of Morphey (see Morphew ).

variants.name: Murphey
meanings: Irish: variant of   Murphy  .
English (mainly northern): variant of Morphey (see Morphew ).

variants.name: Murphree
meanings: Probably Irish: variant of   Murphy  .

variants.name: Morahan
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murcháin ‘descendant of Murchán’, a personal name, a pet form of Murchadh ‘sea-warrior’. See Murphy .

nameMeanings.name: Jeanette
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jeannette  , in the 1920s and 30s associated particularly with the Americam singer and film star  Jeanette MacDonald  (  1902–65 ).

variants.name: Jeannette
meanings: diminutive form of Jeanne, feminine form of Jean ‘John’, now also used in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanette Murphy was born in 1480, in Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Timothy Murphy, was 20 and her mother, Mary O'Connor, was 20. She married John Halsnoth Harsnett in 1515, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1556, in Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanette Murphy est née en 1480, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Timothy Murphy, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Mary O'Connor, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé John Halsnoth Harsnett en 1515, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1556, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.
